scope.summary:
OPERATOR PRODUCT-VISION STEER (2026-06-27): the worksurface is notes editors (MT-097) WITH A CHATBOX BESIDE IT — the Cursor / VS-Code / Antigravity editor+chat layout. This MT adds the CHAT panel as a first-class pane docked beside the editors so the fresh default worksurface shows EDITOR(S) + CHAT side by side.

VERIFIED STATE (live-app inspection 2026-06-27): there is NO existing native chat pane (no runtime_chat / ChatPane surface in src/frontend/handshake_native). A chat surface EXISTS only in the legacy React app via Tauri IPC (app/src/components/swarm/OperatorChat.tsx + app/src-tauri kernel_swarm_chat_generate / session_chat_append / session_chat_read), NOT as a backend HTTP route. There is NO /chat or /conversation HTTP endpoint on handshake_core (verified absent in src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/mod.rs). So the SEND/RECEIVE ROUND-TRIP backend route over the native HTTP client is GENUINELY ABSENT.

What to build (a REAL chat pane scaffold wired to the real route IF one exists, else a HONEST typed blocker — NOT a mock that fakes responses):
- CREATE src/frontend/handshake_native/src/runtime_chat/ (mod.rs + panel.rs): a `ChatPanel` egui widget — a scrollback transcript (a Vec<ChatTurn{role, text}>) + a multiline input box + a Send button. Theme tokens only (no Color32 outside theme/palette.rs — CONTROL-4). AccessKit: register the pane container as Role::Region with author_id = "runtime-chat-panel", the input as `runtime-chat-input` (TextInput), and the send button as `runtime-chat-send` (Button) so a no-context model + swarm agents can drive it (HBR-VIS/SWARM).
- CREATE `ChatPaneFactory` (impl crate::pane_registry::PaneFactory) following the editor_pane_factories.rs MT-079 host-mount pattern (a thin factory whose &self render drives a shared Arc<Mutex<ChatPanel>> the shell also holds). Add a `PaneType::RuntimeChat` variant to pane_registry.rs PaneType (arms in BOTH `label()` => "Chat" and `default_label()` => "Chat"; the compiler enforces exhaustiveness — do not miss one). Register the real ChatPaneFactory in app.rs over the PaneType::RuntimeChat placeholder (alongside install_editor_mounts).
- WIRE THE SEND PATH to the REAL backend route: inspect backend_client.rs for an existing reusable client; the BACKEND HTTP CHAT ROUTE IS VERIFIED ABSENT, so the Send action records a TYPED BLOCKER (a typed `ChatSendError::EndpointMissing` returned from a `RuntimeChatClient` whose `production()` ctor documents the absent route), and the panel renders an HONEST empty-state / disabled-send banner ('Chat backend route absent — wired when handshake_core exposes POST /chat or the IPC bridge is reachable'). DO NOT fabricate an assistant reply. If, on inspection, a reachable HTTP send/receive route is found (e.g. a /jobs-backed or new chat route), wire the Send -> real POST and round-trip a message instead; prefer the real wire when a route exists.
- EDIT app.rs `default_panes()` (post-MT-097): ADD `("pane-c", PaneType::RuntimeChat)` so the fresh default worksurface is CodeSymbol + LoomWikiPage + RuntimeChat (editor(s) + chat side by side). default_tab_bar_states() auto-tracks. Verify the SplitLayoutWidget renders the three panes side by side (the chat docked beside the editors).
- CREATE src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_runtime_chat_pane.rs.

Reuse notes: the PaneFactory trait + the shared-cell `&self render` threading + the factory-map registration are the EXISTING MT-079 editor-mount pattern (editor_pane_factories.rs is the worked reference). The accessibility registry + theme layer are reused (accessibility/registry.rs, theme/palette.rs). backend_client.rs is the reuse-via-API surface for any real route. src/backend untouched.

Risk if missed: a chat pane that fakes assistant replies (a mock) would violate the no-mock Spec-Realism Gate; an absent route must be an HONEST typed blocker + disabled-send empty-state, not a fabricated round-trip.
forbidden_paths: src/backend/** (reuse via API only — no new backend chat route in this MT)
acceptance_criteria: AC-098-1 (REAL CHAT PANE, prove first): a `ChatPaneFactory` is registered in app.rs over PaneType::RuntimeChat (PlaceholderPaneFactory NO LONGER used for it) — proven by a kittest that drives the live HandshakeApp, mounts/opens the chat pane, and asserts the REAL chat AccessKit subtree renders (author_id='runtime-chat-panel' Region with the runtime-chat-input TextInput + runtime-chat-send Button), NOT a placeholder node. A wgpu screenshot of the mounted chat pane beside the editor(s) is saved to the EXTERNAL artifact root.

AC-098-2 (EDITOR + CHAT SIDE BY SIDE): the FRESH default worksurface shows the code editor + rich editor + chat pane (default_panes seeds CodeSymbol + LoomWikiPage + RuntimeChat) — proven by a kittest asserting all three pane subtrees present in the live default tree and the chat pane renders beside the editors (no starved/blank rect).

AC-098-3 (REAL SEND PATH OR HONEST TYPED BLOCKER): the Send action routes through a real `RuntimeChatClient` (backend_client.rs). EITHER (a) a reachable HTTP send/receive route exists and a message ROUND-TRIPS through the real path (the user's typed message appears as a turn AND a real backend response turn is appended) — proven by a kittest typing into runtime-chat-input + clicking runtime-chat-send + observing the round-trip; OR (b) the backend route is verified ABSENT and the Send returns a typed `EndpointMissing`/`ChatSendError` and the panel shows an HONEST disabled-send empty-state banner — proven by a test asserting the typed-blocker path (NO fabricated assistant reply). Exactly one of (a)/(b), chosen by what the live backend actually exposes (verified ABSENT today => path (b) expected).

AC-098-4: AccessKit nodes present (runtime-chat-panel + runtime-chat-input + runtime-chat-send) so a swarm agent can drive the chat via the MCP set_value/click_widget tools; no perpetual spinner; NO Color32 literal outside theme/palette.rs (CONTROL-4 green); the panel uses theme tokens only.

AC-098-5: adding PaneType::RuntimeChat compiles (arms added in BOTH label() and default_label() in pane_registry.rs — exhaustiveness); `cargo build -p handshake-native` (lib + bins) clean; clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` clean; full suite `-j 2` green; external CARGO_TARGET_DIR; `git ls-files src/**/*.png` empty.
proof_targets: PT-098-A: kittest drives the live HandshakeApp, mounts the chat pane, asserts the REAL chat AccessKit subtree (runtime-chat-panel + input + send, not placeholder) + saves a wgpu screenshot of editor+chat side by side to the external root.
PT-098-B: kittest asserts the fresh default worksurface has all three pane subtrees (code + rich + chat) and the chat pane renders a real body beside the editors.

PT-098-C: kittest proves EITHER a real send round-trip (real backend response turn appended) OR the typed EndpointMissing blocker + honest disabled-send empty-state (no fabricated reply). One or the other, by the live backend.
PT-098-D: AccessKit ids present (panel/input/send); CONTROL-4 no-Color32 green; cargo build --lib --bins clean + clippy -D warnings clean + full suite -j 2 green; git ls-files src/**/*.png empty.
implementation_notes: Use the EXTERNAL CARGO_TARGET_DIR (Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-native-target); NEVER a repo-local target/. Run the full suite with -j 2.

Follow editor_pane_factories.rs (MT-079) as the worked host-mount reference: register ChatPaneFactory in the factory map keyed on the new PaneType::RuntimeChat; the &self render drives a shared Arc<Mutex<ChatPanel>> the shell also holds (so a kittest can drive the SAME chat state the pane shows — the AC-098 proofs need the real panel behind the factory).

HONESTY (no-mock Spec-Realism Gate): the backend chat HTTP route is VERIFIED ABSENT (only Tauri IPC kernel_swarm_chat_generate exists; no /chat on handshake_core). So the Send MUST be a typed blocker + honest disabled-send empty-state, NOT a fabricated assistant reply. Do NOT add a fake response generator. If on inspection a reachable HTTP route is genuinely found, wire the real send/receive instead (prefer the real wire).

The typed blocker is the MT-079/080 EndpointMissing pattern: a `RuntimeChatClient::production()` whose send returns `Err(ChatSendError::EndpointMissing)`; the panel shows the route-absent banner. The user's typed message MAY still appear as a local pending turn, but NO assistant turn is synthesized.
Adding PaneType::RuntimeChat requires arms in BOTH the label() and default_label() match statements in pane_registry.rs (the compiler enforces exhaustiveness — do not miss one).
depends_on MT-097 (the notes-only default must land first; this MT extends default_panes to add the chat pane as pane-c). blocks MT-104 (the manual documents the notes+chat worksurface).
Screenshots ONLY to the external Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-012-mt-098/ root; git ls-files src/**/*.png must stay empty.
Read the KERNEL_BUILDER coder protocol under .GOV/ first; visually inspect the editor+chat screenshot (GLOBAL-INSPECT) before declaring the side-by-side layout correct.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/swarm/OperatorChat.tsx + app/src/lib/sessionChat.ts (the React operator-chat transcript/input is REFERENCE for layout only; this is a native pane, and the native backend chat HTTP route is absent).
binds_backend_api: ATTEMPTED: a real chat send/receive route. VERIFIED ABSENT in handshake_core HTTP (only Tauri IPC kernel_swarm_chat_generate exists). So the send is a typed EndpointMissing blocker + honest disabled-send empty-state until a HTTP chat route (or a reachable IPC bridge) exists. No new backend route built here. PostgreSQL/EventLedger only; no SQLite.

hbr_int_009_tier_obligations.reason: The chat pane is a UI surface with a typed-blocker send path; it does not read or write the Flight Recorder. FR stays as-is/forbidden.

This MT does not emit internal_diagnostics events; it is a chat pane scaffold + a typed EndpointMissing blocker. (A later MT could record a BackendUnreachable diag event on a failed send, but that is not required here.)
No freeze/crash/debris record path is touched by adding a chat pane.

risks: RISK-098-1: the pane fabricates assistant replies (a mock) because the real backend route is absent — violates the no-mock Spec-Realism Gate. Mitigate: AC-098-3 requires EITHER a real round-trip OR a typed EndpointMissing blocker with NO synthesized assistant turn.
RISK-098-2: the pane is built as a widget but never host-mounted, so it never renders in the live app (MT-079 RISK-2 'mounted but not consumed'). Mitigate: AC-098-1 live-app mount proof + screenshot.
RISK-098-3: missing a match arm when adding PaneType::RuntimeChat breaks the build. Mitigate: add arms in both label() and default_label() (compiler-enforced exhaustiveness); AC-098-5.
RISK-098-4: a Color32 literal breaks CONTROL-4. Mitigate: theme tokens only; AC-098-4.
RISK-098-5: the three-pane default starves/blanks a pane in the split. Mitigate: AC-098-2 proves all three render a real body beside each other.
RISK-098-6: a tracked PNG leaks. Mitigate: external-only screenshots; git ls-files src/**/*.png empty (AC-098-5).
minimum_controls: MC-098-1: live-app chat-pane mount + screenshot of editor+chat side by side (AC-098-1/2).
MC-098-2: real send round-trip OR honest typed EndpointMissing blocker (no fabricated reply) (AC-098-3).
MC-098-3: AccessKit ids for swarm steering + CONTROL-4 theme (AC-098-4).
MC-098-4: exhaustiveness + external target + no tracked PNG + full suite -j 2 green (AC-098-5).

research_provenance.source: WP-KERNEL-012 product-vision steer (operator, 2026-06-27); live-app inspection (no native chat pane exists; backend HTTP chat route verified ABSENT, only Tauri IPC kernel_swarm_chat_generate + session_chat_* in app/src-tauri).
research_provenance.field_stack: Cursor / VS Code (Copilot Chat) / Antigravity editor+chat docked-pane layout: a chat panel as a first-class dockable pane beside the editor work surface. Reuses the WP-011/MT-079 PaneFactory host-mount pattern + the accessibility registry + theme layer + backend_client.rs reuse-via-API surface. No new subsystem.
research_provenance.motivating_incident: Operator product vision: Handshake should work like Obsidian + VS Code / Cursor / Antigravity — a work surface (notes) with a chatbox beside it. The native frontend has no chat pane today.
research_provenance.validation_plan: live-app chat-pane mount + screenshot (editor+chat side by side) + real send round-trip OR typed EndpointMissing blocker (no fabricated reply) + AccessKit ids + CONTROL-4; clippy -D warnings; full suite -j 2.
